ships_in: Capacity/scaling numbers measured on declared GKE hardware (2026-07-10) and set here; status flipped provisional -> declared. Correctness floors were already enforced in v0.11.
status_note: DECLARED: the capacity/scaling targets below are derived from a real baseline run on the declared hardware class (see measured_on + measurements). ADR-051 §7 requires SLO numbers be measured, not asserted — these are, on real GKE machines. The floor hardware is the WEAKER of the two classes measured (e2-standard-8), so a run on that class or better clears the floors (c3-standard-8, measured faster, also clears them). The gate ENFORCES every non-null target and the correctness floors; run it on the declared hardware (shared CI runners are not release-representative — CI runs slo_gate --selftest only).
measured_on: 2026-07-10; GKE us-central1-a cluster mcp-re-fleet; tls_load_harness_bench (async_serve); cold TLS1.3-mTLS, concurrency 64, 2000 requests/run; MCP_RE_LOADGEN_CORES 1 then 8; in-process echo inner, in-memory replay (envelope-conformant)

correctness_floors.note: Non-capacity invariants — enforced on every run regardless of status (they need no hardware baseline). A run that drops requests or fails closed spuriously fails the gate immediately.

capacity_targets.note: Derived from the DECLARED (weaker) hardware e2-standard-8 at 8 cores: throughput floor ~= 0.64x measured median (390 rps -> 250 floor); latency ceilings ~= 1.5-1.8x measured tail, with margin for run-to-run variance. Both measured classes clear these.

scaling_targets.note: Per-core linear-scaling tolerance for the 1->N cores curve: achieved_N_core_throughput / (single_core_throughput * N) must be >= per_core_min_linear_factor. Floor set below the WEAKER measured factor (c3 0.662, e2 0.718) with margin.

measurements.note: Raw measured baselines (no fabrication). throughput in verified responses/sec; latency in microseconds; scaling = tput_8 / (tput_1 * 8).
